warnings: Absolute pKa is highly sensitive to the G(H+,aq) reference (~1.4 unit shift between Tissandier 1998 and Kelly 2006). Prefer mode='reference' against a known acid in the same family.
HA: 1 imaginary mode(s) — not a true minimum; pKa is approximate.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Method provenance and key details
Property: Aqueous pKa (acid dissociation constant, dimensionless, 298.15 K)
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) with ALPB implicit water (continuum) 
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates for the protonated (HA, neutral) and deprotonated (A⁻, charge −1) forms of trifluoroacetic acid; each structure was pre-optimized and vibrationally analyzed at the same GFN2-xTB/ALPB level by chemkit’s `pka-acidity` workflow (single-conformer, no additional conformer search).  HA shows one soft (<50 i cm⁻¹) imaginary mode, treated as a floppiness rather than a true saddle; A⁻ is a genuine minimum (0 imaginary modes).
Solvent / environment: ALPB implicit water (continuum)
Charge & multiplicity: HA 0/1, A⁻ −1/1 (both closed-shell singlets)
Software: xtb (GFN2-xTB) through chemkit v⟂ (backend version not explicitly reported by engine)
Convergence & caveats: Both species converged.  HA has one soft imaginary mode; see warnings below.  Absolute pKa mode relies on the Tissandier 1998 experimental hydration free energy of H⁺ (−270.28 kcal mol⁻¹) and is suitable only for qualitative screening.

Result
GFN2-xTB absolute aqueous pKa (trifluoroacetic acid) = −131.20166

Live log & files
• Result JSON written to /var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmp6fh07cwm/live.json  
• The xtb run streamed verbosely to a live log (<pka_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS>.out) in the working directory; you can inspect it or replay with the `cli_invocation` recorded in the JSON.

Caveats
xtb absolute pKa values are **not quantitatively accurate** (methodological uncertainty ≥ ~20 pKa units) and should be used for trend screening only, not for direct comparison to experiment.

Warnings (verbatim from engine)
1. Absolute pKa is highly sensitive to the G(H+,aq) reference (~1.4 unit shift between Tissandier 1998 and Kelly 2006). Prefer mode='reference' against a known acid in the same family.
2. HA: 1 imaginary mode(s) — not a true minimum; pKa is approximate.
3. Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.
